problem.title: Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node II
problem.description: Given a binary tree, populate each next pointer to point to its next right node.

example.output: Same next-pointer population but for arbitrary binary tree
example.explanation: Not a perfect binary tree. Use level-order or dummy-head trick to traverse current level and wire up next level.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Level Order Traversal
solutions.description: Traverse the tree level by level using the already-set next pointers of the current level to iterate across it, while building the next level's next-pointer chain with a prev pointer. This avoids a queue and achieves O(1) extra space even for an arbitrary (non-perfect) binary tree.
